summary: Add an explicit off/running lifecycle controller around MCP stdio framing and JSON-RPC dispatch without claiming a desktop process or socket.

decisions.decision: Model MCP as an explicit off/running controller that drops input while off, clears partial frames on stop, and emits newline-delimited responses only while running.
decisions.rationale: This creates a deterministic seam for the eventual desktop child-process lifecycle and proves the toggle-off invariant without opening a local socket or pretending web availability.

testEvidence.criterion: The controller enforces off/running lifecycle and routes framed JSON-RPC requests without losing partial-frame state.
testEvidence.test: Run focused controller/handler tests once, lint once, and production build once.

red.outcome: No red run was required; tests were authored against the existing framing and dispatcher contracts before implementation.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/application/mcp/mcp-stdio-controller.test.ts tests/application/mcp/workbench-mcp-handler.test.ts --reporter=dot; pnpm.cmd lint; pnpm.cmd build
green.outcome: GREEN: 2 test files and 5 tests passed; lint/source policy passed; TypeScript and Vite production build passed with 235 modules transformed.

questions: The actual desktop child process, settings toggle, process inspection proof, and hosted AC-11.c evidence remain pending.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
